TLDR MARKETING 2026-03-24

 AI IS DOING TO GOOGLE WHAT GOOGLE DID TO YAHOO. IS YOUR SEARCH
STRATEGY KEEPING UP? (SPONSOR) [4] 

 Is your search strategy stuck in 2020? Peec AI [4] helps companies
understand AI visibility across models, locations, and topics. Brands
like ATTIO, HUGO BOSS, and N8N use it to:

 	* See when and how they appear in AI answers
 	* Track performance for topics like GEO and AEO
 	* Understand sentiment and how to improve it

Identify the prompts that matter. Monitor your rankings. Act before
your competitors do [4].

вҶ’ See your AI visibility [4]

рҹ“ұ 

NEWS & TRENDS

 AI IS SQUEEZING MARKETING AGENCIES FROM BOTH SIDES (6 MINUTE READ)
[5] 

 Clients expect AI-driven cost savings to reduce their retainers,
while agencies that refuse to adopt AI risk falling behind competitors
and in-house teams. 53% of agency owners now see AI as a significant
threat, and 66% worry that junior team members will have fewer career
opportunities as AI automates the foundational tasks that once served
as training grounds. Larger and more specialized agencies are faring
better, while generalist shops struggle most. 

 BIG TECH'S STRATEGY FOR SELLING AI: DOGFOODING (3 MINUTE READ) [6] 

 Big Tech is using AI internally to sell it externally, making
executives and employees the first users to signal value. At Meta,
Nvidia, and Salesforce, leaders tie performance to AI usage while
marketing presents this as proof that it works. Spending on AI is
rising, yet productivity gains remain modest, with about 40% of adults
using AI at work and saving only 2% of hours, and 9% of roles fully
replaced. Companies adopt norms set by industry leaders, increasing
demand, while massive investments in data centers, chips, and power
push firms to justify usage and accelerate adoption. 

рҹҡҖ 

STRATEGIES & TACTICS

 HOW HUBSPOT SOLVED THE TWO-DATE PROBLEM (1 MINUTE READ) [7] 

 Showing both publish and updated dates can hurt CTR if Google
surfaces the older one in search results. HubSpot avoids this by
displaying only the updated date in standard format while listing the
original publish timing as plain text, which Google ignores. This
keeps search listings looking fresh while still giving readers
context, helping maintain SEO performance. 

 THE BACKSTORY BEHIND CLAY'S NEW PRICING MODEL (5 MINUTE READ) [8] 

 Clay updated its pricing to reflect platform use beyond data, adding
an вҖңactionsвҖқ metric alongside data credits and reducing tiers from
5 to 4. The team spent a year testing with agencies and customer
segments to ensure fairness. Actions remain invisible for most users
while guiding power users to upgrade. Transparent communication
included videos, memos, and grandfathering legacy customers.
Short-term revenue may drop 10%, but long-term upgrades are expected
as action usage grows. 

 VECTOR'S ORGANIC CONTENT STRATEGY BREAKDOWN (4 MINUTE READ) [9] 

 Vector drives growth with a system that turns founder insights into
high-volume content. The team runs biweekly interviews and uses AI
trained on each founder's voice to create LinkedIn posts. Founders
stay active and post consistently. Engagement is the main lever, with
58% of follower growth coming from daily comments on relevant posts.
The company page focuses on employee content. A podcast built on real
internal discussions fuels the strategy, influencing 80% of open
opportunities and serving as source material for posts 

рҹ§‘вҖҚрҹ’» 

RESOURCES & TOOLS

 YOUR EMAILS ARE LANDING IN SPAM MORE OFTEN THAN YOU THINK (SPONSOR)
[10] 

 If you send 100 emails today, how many actually reach the inbox?
Spoiler: not many! 1 in 5 emails never hits the inbox. Warmy.io [11]
improves deliverability automatically, so your emails reach the
primary inbox. Reach prospects. Not spam. [11] 

 THE SCIENCE OF HOW AI PICKS ITS SOURCES (8 MINUTE READ) [12] 

 AI citations are concentrated among a few domains, with content
breadth and structure outweighing rankings. About 30 domains capture
67% of citations, and top Google results are cited only 43.2% of the
time. ChatGPT retrieves many sources but cites few, with 85% of pages
never cited and 67% of cited URLs appearing only once. Longer,
structured content covering multiple queries performs best, and
placing key information in the first 30% of a page maximizes
attention. 

 SEARCH CONSOLE'S AVERAGE POSITION, EXPLAINED (2 MINUTE READ) [13] 

 Average position in GSC is an aggregated metric that often misleads
and offers little actionable insight, so it is usually best ignored.
